changes.detail: Branch does not exist yet. Prompt lines 44-52 instruct Cursor to create it as Stage 0 prerequisite. Cursor must run git checkout -b feature/domain-score-assembler-wave1 before any file modification.

Correct. Sprint creates a new assembler in backend/core/analytics/ and extends AnalysisDTO — both are explicit HIGH-risk paths per SOP §3 (Intelligence Core) and §10. HIGH requires Claude audit + GPT review + dual approval before merge.

Correct. Sprint creates a new analytics module that extends the analysis pipeline output and AnalysisDTO contract. Touches Intelligence Core components. BEHAVIOUR controls apply — MIXED would also be defensible but BEHAVIOUR is sufficient given no new SSOT/YAML assets are created.

changes.check: Authority path — AGENTS.md (READ-ONLY — must not be committed)
changes.detail: SOP v1.3.1 non-negotiable: no tooling-file leakage (AGENTS.md) into governed sprint branches. Cursor must read AGENTS.md as background context only. It must NOT appear in git diff at sprint finish. If Cursor is about to commit AGENTS.md, that is a STOP condition.

changes.citation: Glob and Grep across docs/ and backend/ssot/ — no duplicate domain score authority detected

changes.detail: System key is 'cardiovascular'. min_biomarkers_required: 3. Biomarkers: total_cholesterol, ldl_cholesterol, hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, tc_hdl_ratio. Used as base score source for Domain 1 (cardiovascular health).

changes.check: Scoring rail key — metabolic (blood sugar domain base)
changes.detail: System key is 'metabolic'. Biomarkers: glucose, hba1c, insulin. This is the correct base for Domain 2 (blood sugar control). Electrolyte-heavy systems (e.g. kidney) are separate scoring rails and must not bleed into blood sugar domain score.

changes.check: Scoring rail key — liver (liver health domain base)
changes.detail: System key is 'liver'. Biomarkers: alt, ast. This is the correct base for Domain 3 (liver health). The scoring result dict key is 'liver'.

ALLOWED_BURDEN_SYSTEM_IDS contains 'hepatic', not 'liver'. system_capacity_scores (populated from burden engine and stored on InsightGraphV1, orchestrator.py:1702-1703) uses key 'hepatic'. Cursor MUST use system_capacity_scores.get('hepatic') for burden/capacity context in the liver health assembler. Calling system_capacity_scores['liver'] will silently return None/KeyError and produce wrong confidence without raising a clear error. This is the exact risk the prompt calls out at line 196 and it is real — verified against source.

changes.check: AnalysisDTO frozen model — new field must be Pydantic-declared
changes.detail: AnalysisDTO has model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Adding consumer_domain_scores requires declaring it as a proper Optional Pydantic field (e.g., consumer_domain_scores: Optional[List[ConsumerDomainScoreV1]] = Field(default=None, ...)). Any attempt to inject it as an extra dict key or kwargs will raise ValidationError at runtime. The companion domain score model must also be a frozen Pydantic model with extra='forbid'. This is not a blocker but must be implemented correctly.

changes.check: Signal IDs availability for active_signal_ids field
changes.detail: InsightGraphV1.signal_results is List[Dict[str,Any]] where each item carries signal_id (confirmed at signal_evaluator.py:51). Assembler can iterate signal_results and filter by domain-relevant primary_metric or system mapping to produce active_signal_ids. This is a read-only access path — no modification to signal_results or InsightGraphV1 required.

changes.check: IDL record IDs for primary_idl_record_id field
changes.detail: IDL records expose internal_id (ph_* pattern). Wave 1 relevant IDs: cardiovascular — ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1, ph_lipid_residual_ldl_favourable_transport_v1; blood sugar — ph_metabolic_early_ir_v1, ph_hba1c_metabolic_stress_v1; liver — ph_hepatic_alt_inflammatory_v1. Assembler should look up fired IDL records from AnalysisDTO.interpretation_display_layer_v1. primary_idl_record_id is nullable — emit null if no matching IDL fired.

changes.check: No prediabetes override path exists in analytics core
changes.citation: Grep for 'prediabetes' across backend/core/analytics/ — no matches found
changes.detail: Prompt constraint at line 179 is verified against source. There is no governed prediabetes override implementation path. Cursor must not invent one. If blood sugar confidence cannot be assembled without inventing unsupported logic, STOP condition 3 applies: stop and report the exact blocker.

InsightGraph is built at Step 4.6 (~line 1309). system_capacity_scores is finalised after the burden/propagation steps (~line 1633). The domain score assembler should be invoked after both are available — approximately in the final assembly block (~line 2200+) where AnalysisResult is constructed. Must not alter the burden engine, scoring engine, or insight_graph_builder steps.

build_analysis_result_dto() constructs the API response. consumer_domain_scores should be included in the builder output for API availability (backend-only contract). It must not be wired to any frontend component or results page. STOP condition 2 applies if frontend code alteration is required.

Scope is precisely bounded: three Wave 1 domains, new assembler module, AnalysisDTO extension, targeted backend tests. Explicitly out of scope: frontend, narrative sentences, thyroid, kidney, second-wave domains, LLM.

Key regression targets: test_scoring_policy_not_hardcoded.py (scoring SSOT purity), test_no_raw_fields_in_insight_graph.py (InsightGraph contract), test_runtime_requires_insight_graph.py, test_scoring_orchestrator_integration.py. Sprint must not break these. Cursor should run the targeted suite declared in the prompt — not the full repository test suite.

Assembler sits above the existing scoring/burden/signal layers and reads outputs only. It must not modify scoring engine, burden engine, or phenotype engine behaviour. Any existing panel that doesn't match a Wave 1 domain is unaffected. Output structure of existing fields is unchanged — new field is additive and Optional.

changes.citation: run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py not referenced in prompt scope
changes.detail: Not required for this sprint. If Cursor attempts to modify these files, that is a scope violation and STOP condition.

advisory_notes: The liver/hepatic key split is the single highest-risk implementation point in this sprint. Source-verified: scoring_policy.yaml uses 'liver' for the scoring rail; system_burden_engine.py uses 'hepatic' for burden/capacity. Cursor must map these explicitly in code with inline comments documenting the split.

AnalysisDTO.model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid') — the new ConsumerDomainScoreV1 dataclass or Pydantic model and the Optional field on AnalysisDTO must be declared before the AnalysisResult/AnalysisDTO is instantiated in the orchestrator. A runtime ValidationError here would be a hard crash.

IDL Layer3 system_pressure cards relevant to Wave 1: cardiovascular__system_pressure, metabolic__system_pressure, hepatic__system_pressure (confirmed in docs/DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md:52-57). These may be useful as additional evidence references for the raw evidence block.

Blood sugar confidence: glucose and hba1c are required; insulin is optional enhancement. Triglycerides and TyG-index are derived markers accessible via InsightGraphV1.derived_ratios_meta. Cursor may use these as confidence enhancers but must not blend them silently into the numeric domain score.
AGENTS.md read-only constraint is a hard non-negotiable. It must not appear in the sprint git diff. Stage 4A closure protocol must verify this explicitly before finish.

The sprint reporting requirements (lines 339-390) are comprehensive. Cursor must confirm: (1) branch name, (2) explicit score source and confidence source per domain, (3) calibration handling especially liver/hepatic, (4) exact tests run with results, (5) that work is not merged to main.
